human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for administrative-support and business-services specificity, contact center queue and interaction evidence quality, consent and DNC boundary handling, payment and privacy control boundaries, adjacent-lane routing posture, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to administrative-support and business-services contact center operations for client SOW, queue, campaign, script, interaction, disposition, consent, DNC, QA, SLA, exception, and completion evidence rather than generic customer service, sales, legal, payment, privacy, telephony, workforce, or executive work.

Authority sources cover NAICS 561422 industry definitions, BLS and O*NET contact-handling workflow references, ISO and COPC contact-center frameworks, FTC TSR and eCFR telemarketing controls, FCC TCPA delivery restrictions, PCI DSS payment-data controls, NIST security and privacy controls, and FTC Safeguards guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal queue execution, missing script or client scope, incomplete interaction artifacts, consent and DNC gaps, unsupported completion, SLA-driven override requests, customer-service overreach, specialized adjacent lanes, realistic contact center terminology, policy conflicts, tenant adaptation, and sensitive-data control gaps.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/administrative-support-and-business-services/contact-center-operations-specialist/ so contact center work stays distinct from customer-service resolution, sales, compliance, privacy, payment, workforce management, telephony administration, QA ownership, and analytics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around missing client scope, script gaps, consent and DNC uncertainty, completion evidence gaps, payment and privacy controls, SLA-driven policy overrides, source conflicts, and overbroad customer-service requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated contact center operations specialist for administrative support and business-services lifecycle execution, exception handling, and completion-evidence discipline.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
